_meta.dna: evidence(chain) = capture(screenshot) x hash(sha256) x chain(append) x retain(90d) x query(search) x export(bundle) x tamper(detect)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor has 7 seed questions. Questions must be EXECUTABLE — answerable by checking if something actually works, not theoretical capability.

name: Tower of Evidence Chains
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(7q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_context: 33, San Francisco, Product liability lawyer at a tech company. Expert tech level. Needs automatic screenshots at every critical step of an AI agent's browser actions — for litigation defense if a customer claims the agent did something unauthorized.
questions: Does the browser automatically capture a full-page screenshot before and after every click, form submission, and navigation action?
Can I see the screenshots in chronological order as a visual timeline of everything the agent did during a session?
Does each screenshot include metadata — the URL, timestamp, DOM state hash, and the recipe step number that triggered it?
Can I zoom into a specific screenshot to read small text like confirmation numbers or error messages?
Does the capture include the full page content (not just the visible viewport) so off-screen elements are documented?
Can I export all screenshots from a specific session as a timestamped ZIP for external legal review?
Does the system capture screenshots even when a step fails — so I can see what the page looked like at the moment of failure?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all screenshot capture scenarios. Rachel needs litigation-grade evidence — every agent action must have visual proof. Missing a screenshot at a critical moment is a legal liability gap.

floors.persona_context: 46, Moscow, Blockchain architect consulting for a European bank. Expert tech level. Needs every evidence artifact to be SHA-256 hashed so any tampering is mathematically detectable — the same standard used in blockchain immutability.
questions: Does every screenshot, log entry, and recipe execution record have a SHA-256 hash computed and stored alongside the artifact?
Can I independently verify a hash by downloading the artifact and running sha256sum on my own machine?
Does the system use the artifact's raw bytes for hashing (not a re-encoded version that might differ from the original)?
Can I see the hash of every artifact in the evidence viewer UI without needing to use command-line tools?
Does the hash computation happen immediately at capture time (not in a background batch job that could be interrupted)?
If I request the hash of an artifact via the API, does the response include both the hash and the algorithm identifier (SHA-256)?
Does the system detect and flag if two different artifacts produce the same hash (collision detection for integrity assurance)?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all SHA-256 hashing scenarios. Dmitri needs cryptographic-grade integrity — every hash must be independently verifiable, computed at capture time, and stored immutably. Hash-after-the-fact is not trustworthy.

floors.persona_context: 38, Paris, Compliance engineer at a pharmaceutical manufacturer. Expert tech level. Needs an append-only evidence chain where each entry links to the previous one — any insertion, deletion, or reordering must be impossible and detectable.
questions: Does each new evidence entry include the hash of the previous entry, creating a linked chain that breaks if any entry is modified?
Can I verify the entire chain integrity with a single command that walks from the first entry to the last checking every link?
Does the system prevent deletion of any entry in the chain — even by an admin account?
If I try to insert an entry between two existing entries, does the system reject the insertion and log the attempt?
Can I see the chain visualized as a linked sequence showing the hash linkage between consecutive entries?
Does the chain include a genesis entry (first link) with a known seed hash that serves as the trust anchor?
If the chain is corrupted (a link breaks), does the system identify exactly which entry was tampered with?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all chain append and integrity scenarios. Sophie works in pharma — FDA Part 11 requires append-only audit trails. Any ability to modify, delete, or reorder evidence entries is a regulatory violation.

floors.persona_context: 41, London, Head of operations at an insurance brokerage. Intermediate tech level. Regulators require 90-day retention of all automated decision evidence. Needs to know exactly when evidence expires and whether it can be extended for open claims.
questions: Does the system retain all evidence artifacts for exactly 90 days from creation, with a visible expiration date on each entry?
Can I extend the retention period for specific evidence bundles that are part of an open insurance claim?
Does the system send me an alert 7 days before evidence is about to expire so I can decide whether to extend it?
After 90 days, does the system actually delete the evidence (not just hide it) to comply with data minimization rules?
Can I see a dashboard showing all evidence approaching expiration in the next 30 days, sorted by expiry date?
Does the retention clock start from the evidence creation timestamp (not the recipe creation date)?
If I export an evidence bundle before it expires, does my local copy remain valid and verifiable after the server copy is deleted?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all 90-day retention scenarios. James needs regulatory retention with clear expiration. Evidence must not expire during an active claim, but must be deleted when retention ends (GDPR data minimization).

floors.persona_context: 35, Shanghai, Digital forensics investigator at a consulting firm. Expert tech level. Needs to search across thousands of evidence entries by date, agent, recipe, action type, and keyword — finding the needle in the haystack during an incident investigation.
questions: Can I search evidence entries by date range, agent ID, recipe name, and action type in a single compound query?
Does the search return results within 2 seconds even when scanning 10,000+ evidence entries?
Can I filter evidence by outcome — for example, show me only failed executions in the last 7 days?
Does the search support keyword matching against screenshot OCR text so I can find evidence containing a specific confirmation number?
Can I save a search query as a named filter that I can re-run daily during an ongoing investigation?
Does the search result include a preview thumbnail of each screenshot so I can visually scan without opening each one?
Can I export the search results as a structured JSON or CSV for ingestion into external forensic analysis tools?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all evidence query scenarios. Mei-Ling investigates incidents — she needs fast, precise, compound queries across massive evidence stores. Slow search or missing filter dimensions makes evidence collection impractical during a time-sensitive investigation.

floors.persona_context: 55, Gothenburg, External auditor at a Big Four accounting firm. Expert tech level. Needs to export a self-contained evidence bundle that can be verified offline by regulators who do not have access to the Solace platform.
questions: Can I export a complete evidence bundle as a single ZIP file containing all screenshots, logs, hashes, and the hash chain?
Does the exported bundle include a verification script that the auditor can run on their own machine without installing Solace?
Can the auditor verify the hash chain integrity using only standard tools (Python, OpenSSL, or sha256sum)?
Does the bundle include a human-readable summary (HTML or PDF) alongside the machine-verifiable artifacts?
Can I select a specific date range or set of recipe executions to include in the bundle, rather than exporting everything?
Does the export include the recipe version that was executed, so the auditor can confirm what logic produced the evidence?
Is the exported bundle digitally signed so the auditor can verify it came from the Solace platform and was not modified in transit?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all bundle export scenarios. Anders is an external auditor — he needs a self-contained, offline-verifiable evidence package. If the bundle requires Solace platform access to verify, it fails the independence requirement.

floors.persona_context: 49, Zurich, Chief Risk Officer at a private bank. Expert tech level. Needs mathematical proof that no evidence was altered after capture — if a rogue employee modifies an evidence entry, the system must detect and alert immediately.
questions: If a single byte of a screenshot file is modified after capture, does the system detect the hash mismatch on the next integrity check?
Does the system run automated integrity checks on the evidence chain periodically (not just when manually triggered)?
When tampering is detected, does the system generate an alert with the exact entry that was modified and the expected vs actual hash?
Can I configure tamper alerts to send to multiple recipients (CRO, CISO, external auditor) simultaneously?
Does the tamper detection work even if the attacker modifies both the artifact and its stored hash (because the chain link to the next entry will break)?
Can I run a full chain verification from genesis to latest entry and get a certificate confirming the chain is intact as of this moment?
Does the system log every integrity check — both passed and failed — so I have proof that verification was performed regularly?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all tamper detection scenarios. Ingrid is CRO at a Swiss bank — any undetected evidence modification is a regulatory catastrophe. Tamper detection must be automatic, multi-layered (hash + chain link), and alerting.
